patch.value: SDK authoring DX polish: manifest-drift guard in scaffold tests, expectation key naming, repeated --capability, define* index signatures

Bundle of P3 SDK DX findings from the 2026-07-02 audit (Lane B, pm-6vy7): (1) generated scaffold test should call assertExtensionManifestMatchesBlueprint so manifest.json drift fails the starter suite; (2) test-expectation key drift across helpers (command/itemType/field/targetCommand/provider) is inconsistent; (3) repeated --capability flags silently last-win instead of erroring or composing; (4) [key: string]: unknown index signatures on define* option types weaken excess-property checking.
Scaffold tests include a manifest-drift guard; expectation keys named consistently; --capability is repeatable; define* builders accept index signatures without casts.

## Context
P3 SDK-authoring DX bundle from the 2026-07-02 audit (Lane B, pm-6vy7). Four independent paper-cuts, batched because each is small and they all land in the SDK authoring surface:

1. Scaffolded test should call assertExtensionManifestMatchesBlueprint so manifest.json drift fails the starter suite (author-once guarantee).
2. Test-expectation key naming drifts across helpers (command vs itemType vs field vs targetCommand vs provider) — align on one convention and alias the old keys.
3. Repeated --capability flags silently last-win; they should compose (collect) or error (note PR#82 lesson: collect-repeatable flags must NOT be list:true).
4. [key: string]: unknown index signatures on define* option types defeat excess-property checking — replace with exact optional members so typos fail typecheck.

## Definition of done
Each sub-fix has a spec; scaffold output includes the manifest-drift assertion; contracts:update run if flag semantics change; SDK docs updated for the expectation-key convention.
